source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A rectangular garden is surrounded by a path of constant width. The perimeter of the garden is $24 \mathrm{~m}$ shorter than the distance along the outside edge of the path. What is the width of the path? <image1>

image_dependency: question_text is fully self-contained and does not reference a diagram, figure, chart, or visual attributes
although an image asset exists in the registry, the wording supports solving from text alone
multi_step: solver must relate the outer perimeter of a rectangle with a uniform surrounding path to the inner perimeter
finding the width requires deriving how perimeter changes when width is added on all sides, then solving from the 24 m difference
this is more than direct recall but still a short algebraic/geometry derivation
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a single numeric result ('$3 m$')
the problem condition gives a deterministic equation, so the result can be independently checked
multiple-choice options are provided, making answer matching straightforward

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present, and the registry passed with no recorded issues
the sample is highly verifiable because it has a single short-text numeric answer and deterministic conditions
the problem appears text-self-contained, so the low image dependency does not block recoverability or usability
multi-step reasoning demand is moderate, which supports inclusion in the candidate pool
the only noted risk is that an image is present but not needed, which is a minor ranking issue rather than a reason to defer or reject
